title: VimFu — :[range]sort Options
description: The :sort command has options: n for numeric, u for unique lines, i for case insensitive — and you can combine them, like :sort iu for case-insensitive unique.

setup.comment: Set TERM for proper color support in Neovim
Create demo file with mixed content

setup.content:
item 10
item 2
Apple
apple
item 30
Banana
banana
item 1
Apple
item 20

steps.say: Sort has options. N for numeric. U for unique. I for case insensitive. They combine.
Notice this list isn't sorted — Apple appears three times, Banana twice.

steps.comment: --- Demo 1: regular sort exposes the numeric problem ---

steps.say: First, a plain sort. Colon percent sort.
Item ten came before item two. Alphabetical, not numeric. And every duplicate is still there.
Add N for numeric. Colon percent sort N.
Now items are in numeric order. One, two, ten, twenty, thirty. But Apple still appears three times.

steps.comment: --- Demo 3: add U for unique (case sensitive) ---

steps.say: Combine N and U for numeric unique. Colon percent sort N U.
U dropped one duplicate Apple. But lowercase apple is still separate. U is case sensitive.

steps.comment: --- Demo 4: add I for case insensitive ---

steps.say: Combine I and U for case insensitive unique. Colon percent sort I U.
Now all three Apples merge into one. Banana too. Case insensitive dedup.
Sort N for numbers. U for unique. I for case insensitive. Combine them as needed.
